fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue company with high growth estimates but currently unprofitable and trading at a high forward multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY, with analyst estimates for current Q at 109.4% and current FY at 441.7%.
Forward P/E of 34.37 is supported by a PEG ratio of 0.78, indicating growth is priced in.
Strong demand from data centers and AI computing sectors.
Current ratio of 3.83 and quick ratio of 2.85 indicate good short-term liquidity.
Mediacom deal to upgrade 1 million homes is a significant customer win.
negatives: Operating margin of -8.6% and profit margin of -8.5% indicate the company is not yet profitable.
Return on equity of -6.1% and return on assets of -3.3% show poor returns on capital.
Free cash flow (TTM) is negative at $-445.58M, indicating cash burn.
Last reported quarter missed EPS estimates by 44.8%.
High beta of 3.76 suggests significant stock volatility.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) is experiencing strong demand from data centers and AI computing, with recent reports highlighting a significant deal to upgrade 1 million homes for Mediacom. However, the company also saw a sharp 9% drop in its stock price on May 29th, alongside other optics stocks, as the sector cooled off. Despite the recent pullback, the strong demand and analyst growth estimates suggest a potentially bullish outlook, though the negative margins and recent earnings miss warrant caution.
